2605.09440.title: Key Coverage Matters: Semi-Structured Extraction of OCR Clinical Reports
2605.09440.paper_summary: Clinical reports are often fragmented across healthcare institutions because privacy regulations and data silos limit direct information sharing. When patients seek care at a different hospital, they often carry paper or scanned reports from prior visits. This hinders EHR integration and longitudinal review, and downstream applications that depend on more complete patient records, such as patient management, follow-up care, real-world studies, and clinical-trial matching. Although OCR can digitize such reports, reliable extraction remains challenging because clinical documents are heterogeneous, OCR text is noisy, and many healthcare settings require low-cost on-premise deployment. We formulate this problem as canonical key-conditioned extractive question answering over OCR-derived clinical reports. Because the key fields are neither fixed nor known in advance, the key space is open. We maintain a canonical key inventory through iterative key mining, normalization, clustering, and lightweight human verification, and introduce key coverage as a metric to quantify inventory completeness. Using a 0.2B BERT-based model, experiments on real-world reports from more than 20 hospitals show performance improves monotonically with key coverage. The model achieves F1 scores of 0.839 and 0.893 under exact match and boundary-tolerant matching, respectively, once the Top-90 canonical keys are covered. These results show that key coverage is a dominant factor for end-to-end performance. At Top-90 coverage, our model outperforms a fine-tuned Qwen3-0.6B baseline under exact match. Although our annotated corpus is Chinese, the method relies on the language-agnostic key-value organization of semi-structured clinical reports and can be adapted to other settings given an appropriate canonical key inventory and alias mapping.
2605.09440.paper_summary_zh: 臨床報告在醫療機構之間往往是支離破碎的，因為隱私法規和數據孤島限制了直接的信息共享。當患者在不同的醫院尋求護理時，他們經常攜帶之前就診的紙本或掃描報告。這阻礙了電子健康記錄（EHR）的整合和縱向審查，以及依賴更完整患者記錄的下游應用，例如患者管理、後續護理、真實世界研究和臨床試驗匹配。儘管光學字符識別（OCR）可以數字化這些報告，但可靠的提取仍然具有挑戰性，因為臨床文件是異質的，OCR文本是嘈雜的，並且許多醫療環境需要低成本的本地部署。我們將這個問題表述為基於關鍵條件的經典提取式問題回答，針對OCR衍生的臨床報告。由於關鍵字段既不是固定的，也不是事先已知的，因此關鍵空間是開放的。我們通過迭代的關鍵挖掘、標準化、聚類和輕量級人類驗證來維護經典關鍵庫，並引入關鍵覆蓋作為量化庫完整性的指標。使用一個0.2B的基於BERT的模型，對來自20多家醫院的真實報告進行的實驗顯示，隨著關鍵覆蓋的增加，性能單調提升。當Top-90經典關鍵被覆蓋時，模型在精確匹配和邊界容忍匹配下分別達到0.839和0.893的F1分數。這些結果顯示，關鍵覆蓋是端到端性能的主導因素。在Top-90覆蓋下，我們的模型在精確匹配下超越了微調的Qwen3-0.6B基準。儘管我們的註釋語料庫是中文，但該方法依賴於半結構化臨床報告的語言無關的鍵值組織，並且可以在給定適當的經典關鍵庫和別名映射的情況下適應其他環境。
2605.09440.author: Yu Wang et.al.
2605.09440.authors: Yu Wang, Yingyun Li, Ying Qin, Haiyang Qian
